title: Bot System with Auto-Rotating Camera
content:
## Overview

The snake game supports computer-driven autoplay via a bot system with three selectable profiles. When a bot is active, the isometric camera slowly auto-rotates to make demo/watch mode visually engaging. The system was built across several files under `src/bots/` and extended `src/main.ts` with pathfinding primitives, UI wiring, and the camera loop.

---

## Bot Profiles

Three named profiles are available, each implemented as a separate module in `src/bots/`:

| Profile | File | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| **Survival** | `src/bots/survival-bot.ts` | Prioritizes staying alive; weights open space and tail reachability heavily |
| **Hunter** | `src/bots/hunter-bot.ts` | Aggressively chases food; weights path-to-food distance most |
| **Explorer** | `src/bots/explorer-bot.ts` | Favors open lanes away from walls |

All three use the same scoring formula structure — evaluating candidate moves by `reachableArea`, `canReachTail`, `pathToFood`, and wall proximity — but with different weight distributions that produce meaningfully different play styles.

Shared types (`BotState`, `BotHelpers`, `SnakeBot`, `AnalysisResult`) live in `src/bots/bot-types.ts`. A registry in `src/bots/index.ts` exposes the profile list and a lookup function.

---

## Bot Interface and Isolation

Each bot's decision function receives:
- A **frozen snapshot** of game state (`BotState`)
- A **plain object of helper functions** (`BotHelpers`) assembled fresh each tick

Bots never hold a reference to the game object. This keeps bot logic self-contained and testable in isolation, and prevents accidental mutation of live game state. The coupling is strictly one-directional: the game engine calls the bot, not the reverse.

---

## Pathfinding and Game Engine Primitives

`src/main.ts` provides the infrastructure bots depend on:

### Unified Position Analysis (`analyzePosition`)

A single BFS pass from a candidate position simultaneously computes all three metrics bots need:
- **`reachableArea`** — count of reachable cells (flood-fill)
- **`canReachTail`** — whether any explored cell is adjacent to the tail position (tail cell stays blocked; adjacency detection replaces a direct path check)
- **`pathToFood`** — shortest BFS distance to the food cell

This replaced three separate passes (`countReachableArea`, `hasPath`, `findShortestPathLength`) that previously tripled per-move computation. The queue uses a pointer index instead of `shift()` to avoid O(n) array mutations on every dequeue.

### Other Primitives
- **Move simulator** — returns the resulting snake state after a candidate move, or `null` on collision
- **Reverse-direction filter** — filters the reverse of the current heading from candidate directions
- **`chooseBotDirection()`** — assembles state and helpers, delegates to the active bot each tick

---

## Snake Occupancy Set (`snakeSet`)

A `Set<string>` field (`snakeSet`) is maintained in sync with the `snake` array throughout `src/main.ts`:
- Initialized on game start and grid-size change
- Head key added on each advance; tail key deleted when the snake does not eat
- Used in `checkCollision` and `spawnFood` for O(1) membership tests, replacing O(n) array scans

**Food spawn strategy**: At low fill ratios, the existing random-sample-and-reject loop is preserved (fast on average). Above 50% grid fill, a full-grid scan collects all empty cells and picks randomly from that list, avoiding rejection-sampling degradation at high density.

---

## Shared Direction Constants

`src/game-types.ts` exports `Position`, `Direction`, `DIRECTIONS`, `DIRECTION_VECTORS`, and `OPPOSITE_DIRECTIONS`. Both `src/main.ts` and all bot files import from this module, eliminating the inline duplicates that previously existed in the main file and ensuring bots and the game engine always agree on direction semantics.

---

## UI and Controls

Bot selection and control surfaces appear in two places:
- **Main menu**: profile dropdown, description line, toggle button, "Start Demo Bot" button (launches a new game with the bot pre-enabled)
- **In-game overlay**: profile dropdown (synced with the menu dropdown), description line, toggle button, green status indicator when active

**B key** toggles the bot on/off. Both dropdowns are kept in sync programmatically so switching on either screen takes effect immediately.

**Manual input while bot is active**: Arrow key and WASD presses are silently ignored. This prevents a tug-of-war between player input and bot decisions.

---

## Auto-Rotating Camera

When the bot is enabled, a `requestAnimationFrame` loop increments the isometric camera angle at **0.15 radians per second** and redraws the canvas each frame. The loop starts when the bot is enabled and stops on game over or when the bot is toggled off.

**Manual adjustment behavior**: Pressing Q or E to rotate the camera sets an `autoRotatePausedUntil` timestamp three seconds in the future. The auto-rotate step checks this timestamp and skips angle updates until the cooldown expires — the animation loop itself is never cancelled, so rotation resumes smoothly without any extra interaction.

---

## File Map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/game-types.ts` | Shared `Position`, `Direction`, `DIRECTIONS`, `DIRECTION_VECTORS`, `OPPOSITE_DIRECTIONS` |
| `src/bots/bot-types.ts` | `BotState`, `BotHelpers`, `SnakeBot`, `AnalysisResult` types |
| `src/bots/survival-bot.ts` | Survival profile implementation |
| `src/bots/hunter-bot.ts` | Hunter profile implementation |
| `src/bots/explorer-bot.ts` | Explorer profile implementation |
| `src/bots/index.ts` | Bot registry and lookup |
| `src/main.ts` | Game engine extensions: `analyzePosition`, `snakeSet`, `chooseBotDirection`, auto-rotate loop, UI wiring |
